spec_match.rationale: Touches HTML, URL from the chromestatus summary but skips well-defined, widely-used, ASCII, same-origin — secondary spec surfaces are not exercised.

interactive.rationale: Live UI is real (button, eventListener, input); controls include "Same origin"; "evil.example"; "null (opaque)" — well past the no-static-card bar.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature-index blurb and page H1/lede share the same framing and surface the same API affordances.

edge_cases.rationale: Happy path is solid and some failure/feature-detect paths are surfaced, but the full set of edge cases (timeouts, abort, malformed input, multiple invocations) is not exercised.

references.rationale: see-also section links to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5095541277065216 so the cleanup script can recover this folder.

design_system.rationale: Design-system regressions: button rule overrides background without color (.preset-btn:hover; .method-btn:hover:not(.active)).

openQuestions.title: Surface the missing API surfaces on postmessage-origin-guard
openQuestions.detail: The chromestatus summary for Introducing the Origin API introduces well-defined, widely-used, ASCII, same-origin, same-site but the page does not mention or exercise them. Walk the spec and add a short labelled control or read-out for each, so the concept covers the headline API in full. Spec: https://github.com/whatwg/html/pull/11846

openQuestions.title: Show the failure modes on postmessage-origin-guard
openQuestions.detail: Right now the page only demonstrates the happy path. Add at least one of: a feature-detection probe with graceful fallback, an error/throw path (empty input, invalid argument), and a boundary case (zero, max, abort).

openQuestions.title: Bring postmessage-origin-guard back onto the design system
openQuestions.detail: Design-system regressions: button rule overrides background without color (.preset-btn:hover; .method-btn:hover:not(.active)). See CLAUDE.md rules #5, #6, #7 — CSS variables only, WCAG AA, button background+color always overridden together.

summary: postmessage-origin-guard covers the happy path of Introducing the Origin API and is interactive enough to clear the bar, but spec coverage and edge cases need filling in — see open questions.
